name: Grilled Salmon & Quinoa Bowl
instructions: Season salmon fillets with salt, pepper, and lemon zest.
Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat and brush with oil.
Grill salmon for 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook quinoa in vegetable broth per package directions.
Steam broccoli and asparagus until tender-crisp, about 5 minutes.
Assemble bowls: quinoa base, topped with salmon and vegetables.
Drizzle with a tahini-lemon dressing and serve immediately.

name: Mediterranean Chicken & Veggie Tray
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Toss chicken thighs with olive oil, garlic, oregano, and lemon juice.
Arrange chicken, bell peppers, zucchini, and cherry tomatoes on a sheet pan.
Roast for 35–40 minutes until chicken is golden and cooked through.
Scatter kalamata olives and crumbled feta over the tray in the last 5 minutes.
Serve with warm pita or over cauliflower rice.

name: Black Bean & Sweet Potato Tacos
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cube sweet potato and toss with cumin and olive oil.
Roast sweet potato cubes for 20 minutes until caramelized.
Warm black beans in a small saucepan with garlic and smoked paprika.
Warm corn tortillas directly over a gas flame or in a dry skillet.
Assemble tacos: beans, sweet potato, shredded cabbage, and avocado.
Top with lime juice, fresh cilantro, and a drizzle of hot sauce.
Serve immediately with lime wedges.

name: Zucchini Noodles with Pesto & Shrimp
instructions: Spiralize zucchini into noodles using a spiralizer or vegetable peeler.
Pat shrimp dry and season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Sauté shrimp for 2 minutes per side until pink; remove and set aside.
Add zucchini noodles to the same skillet and toss for 1–2 minutes (don't overcook).
Remove from heat, toss with basil pesto and cherry tomatoes.
Return shrimp to pan, toss to combine, and serve with Parmesan.

name: Cauliflower Fried Rice
instructions: Pulse cauliflower florets in a food processor until rice-sized pieces form.
Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Scramble eggs in the pan, then push to the side.
Add diced carrots, peas, and green onions; stir-fry 3 minutes.
Add cauliflower rice and stir-fry 4–5 minutes until slightly golden.
Season with soy sauce, ginger, and a dash of white pepper.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve immediately.

name: Lettuce-Wrapped Turkey Burgers
instructions: Combine ground turkey with diced onion, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, and seasoning.
Form into 2 burger patties, pressing a dimple in the center.
Heat a cast-iron pan over medium-high heat with a little oil.
Cook burgers 5–6 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 165°F.
Layer toppings: tomato, red onion, avocado, and a dollop of Dijon mustard.
Wrap each burger in 2–3 large iceberg lettuce leaves instead of a bun.
Serve with a side of sliced cucumber.

name: Classic Smash Burgers
instructions: Divide ground beef into 4 loose balls (don't pack tightly).
Heat a cast-iron skillet or griddle over very high heat until smoking.
Place beef balls on the skillet and immediately smash flat with a spatula.
Season generously with salt and pepper. Cook 2–3 minutes until edges are lacy.
Flip once, add a slice of American cheese, and cook 1 minute more.
Stack two patties per burger on a toasted brioche bun.
Load up with special sauce, pickles, white onion, and lettuce.

name: Loaded Mac & Cheese with Bacon
instructions: Cook elbow pasta in salted boiling water until al dente; reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Fry bacon strips until crispy; crumble and set aside, reserving 1 tbsp fat.
Make roux: melt butter in a large saucepan, whisk in flour, cook 1 minute.
Whisk in milk gradually until smooth. Simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in cheddar and Gruyère until melted and smooth.
Fold in pasta, adding pasta water to loosen if needed.
Top with bacon, breadcrumbs, and a sprinkle of paprika. Broil 2 minutes.

name: Pepperoni Deep Dish Pizza
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Grease a 9-inch cast-iron skillet generously with butter.
Press store-bought pizza dough into and up the sides of the skillet.
Layer mozzarella slices directly on the dough (cheese goes first!).
Add a generous layer of pepperoni on top of the cheese.
Spoon crushed San Marzano tomatoes over pepperoni, season with oregano.
Top with grated Parmesan and another layer of pepperoni.
Bake 25–30 minutes until crust is golden brown. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.

name: Egg White & Spinach Frittata
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Whisk egg whites with a pinch of salt until slightly frothy.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Sauté diced onion and garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes. Season with nutmeg.
Add diced roasted red peppers and distribute evenly.
Pour egg whites over the vegetables. Cook undisturbed 2 minutes until edges set.
Transfer to oven and bake 10–12 minutes until fully set. Slice and serve.

name: Spicy Peanut Soup with Sesame Halloumi
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté the shallots and garlic for 1–2 minutes until softened.
Add the Thai red curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the vegetable broth, peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, and diced sweet potato.
Bring to a boil then simmer for 10–15 minutes until the sweet potato is tender.
Meanwhile, slice the halloumi and pan-fry in a dry skillet over medium-high heat until golden on both sides, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Ladle the soup into bowls, top with sesame halloumi, crushed peanuts, and a squeeze of lime.

name: Shakshuka
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion for about 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook until the tomato paste darkens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Add the sliced spicy sausage (if using) and cook until lightly browned.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, diced tomatoes, salt, pepper, and 150 ml water. Simmer for 10–15 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook for 2–3 minutes until the whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Serve directly from the pan with crusty bread or pita.

name: Crispy Rice Cucumber Salad with Lemon Tahini Dressing
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions and let cool completely.
Spread the cooled rice on a baking sheet and drizzle with soy sauce, crispy chili oil, and sesame oil. Toss to coat.
Roast at 200°C for 30–40 minutes, tossing halfway, until golden and crispy.
Slice cucumbers, dice avocado, and prepare spring onions. Thaw edamame.
Make the dressing: whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, tahini, maple syrup, minced garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper. Add a splash of water to thin if needed.
Combine the salad vegetables in a large bowl. Add the crispy rice and toss with the dressing.
Top with salted peanuts and serve immediately.

name: Jennifer Aniston Salad
instructions: Make the dressing: whisk together olive oil, minced garlic, honey, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Cook quinoa according to package directions. Fluff with a fork and let cool.
Dice cucumbers and finely chop the red onion. Drain and rinse the chickpeas.
Combine quinoa, cucumbers, red onion, chickpeas, fresh mint, and pistachios in a large bowl.
Add the dressing and toss to combine. Crumble feta over the top and serve.

name: Glow Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C. Chop sweet potato and broccoli. Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, dried thyme, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking tray.
Spread canned lentils and pumpkin seeds on a second baking tray. Add halved Medjool dates and a pinch of salt.
Roast both trays for 35–40 minutes until golden.
In a large bowl, combine arugula, sliced avocado, lemon juice, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Make the dressing: whisk together tahini, turmeric, sriracha, apple cider vinegar, agave syrup, salt, pepper, and enough water to make it pourable.
Add the roasted vegetables and lentils to the salad. Drizzle with the tahini dressing and serve.

name: Fried Rice with Crispy Tofu
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water 3–4 times until the water runs clearer. Cook 3 minutes less than the package instructions.
Roughly chop the shallots and garlic. Blend with one-third of the oil using a hand blender until a smooth paste forms. Slice spring onions into thin rings.

Cut tofu into 1.5 cm cubes. Heat a sixth of the oil in a frying pan over high heat. Fry the tofu for 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until light brown and crispy. In the last minute, stir in the fried onions.
Heat the remaining oil in a wok over medium heat. Fry the shallot paste for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the rice, frozen peas, and spring onions. Stir-fry for 3 minutes on high heat until well mixed.
Add the sweet soy sauce and soy sauce. Mix well. Serve topped with crispy tofu.

name: Baked Pasta Casserole
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Dice the zucchini, eggplant, and shallot into chunks.
Place all the vegetables into a large baking dish.
Pour the tomato basil sauce over the vegetables, followed by the vegetable broth.
Season with Italian herbs, salt, and pepper. Add the dry fusilli and stir to combine.
Cover tightly with aluminium foil and bake for 40 minutes until the pasta is cooked and the sauce is absorbed.
Serve hot, optionally topped with chicken meatballs baked separately.

name: Halloumi Quinoa Salad
instructions: Cook quinoa according to package directions. Fluff and set aside.
Preheat oven to 190°C. Chop sweet potato and broccoli. Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper. Roast for 20 minutes until golden.
Slice halloumi and pan-fry in a dry skillet, drizzling with honey, until golden on both sides.
Make the dressing: whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, minced garlic, lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Arrange arugula and quinoa in bowls. Top with roasted vegetables, halloumi, pomegranate seeds, pumpkin seeds, and sunflower seeds. Drizzle with dressing.

name: Lima Beans in Tomato Cream Sauce
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a pan. Stir in the breadcrumbs, season with salt and pepper, and cook stirring regularly until golden brown. Set aside to cool.
In the same pan, heat 2 more tbsp olive oil. Add the finely chopped onion and garlic, season with salt and pepper, and cook for about 4 minutes.
Add the tomato paste and cook for another 3 minutes.
Add the drained lima beans, cream, finely chopped sun-dried tomatoes, 30 g Parmesan, and 80 ml water. Simmer for 5–8 minutes.
Make the arugula salad: toss arugula with toasted breadcrumbs, lemon zest, lemon juice, and the remaining Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve the beans alongside the arugula salad.

name: Green Goddess Soup
instructions: Chop all vegetables into three groups — Group 1: onion, leek, fennel, celery, potato. Group 2: garlic and ginger. Group 3: broccoli, peas, and spinach.
Heat olive oil in a large soup pot. Add Group 1 with a pinch of salt. Cook for 10 minutes until all vegetables are soft.
Add Group 2 along with the cumin and black pepper. Cook for 2 more minutes.
Dissolve the bouillon cubes and miso paste in 250 ml hot water. Add to the pot along with about 1 L water until all vegetables are submerged.
Add Group 3 and bring to a boil with the lid on until all vegetables are tender.
Blend the soup with an immersion blender until smooth. Adjust seasoning.
Serve topped with pumpkin seeds, crispy chili oil, and an optional drizzle of coconut cream.

name: Baked Gnocchi with Roasted Eggplant & Ricotta
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C. Place halved cherry tomatoes, diced eggplant, garlic cloves, olives, and balsamic vinegar in a baking dish. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Cover with foil and roast for 20 minutes, then uncover and roast for another 20 minutes until caramelized.
Mix the ricotta with lemon zest, grated Parmesan, salt, and pepper.
Once the vegetables are done, mash roughly with a fork, adding a splash of water if needed. Stir in fresh basil.
Cook the gnocchi according to package directions. Drain and add to the roasted vegetable sauce in the baking dish. Toss to combine and serve immediately.

name: Sesame Crusted Tofu with Rice
instructions: Make the peanut-tahini sauce: whisk together tahini, peanut butter, sesame oil, maple syrup, grated ginger, minced garlic, salt, pepper, and enough water to reach a dippable consistency.
Cut the tofu into flat rectangles. Dip each piece in the sauce, then roll in sesame seeds to coat.
Bake the coated tofu at 200°C for 20–30 minutes until golden and crispy.
Make the cucumber salad: thinly slice mini cucumbers and spring onions. Toss with a drizzle of sesame oil, a pinch of salt, and sesame seeds.
Cook the rice according to package directions.
Serve the sesame tofu over rice with the cucumber salad alongside.

name: Quinoa Salad
instructions: Cook quinoa according to package directions. Fluff and let cool.
Dice cucumber, slice radishes, chop red bell pepper, and halve cherry tomatoes.
Make the dressing: whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, honey, minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Combine all vegetables, quinoa, chickpeas, olives, mint, and pomegranate seeds in a large bowl.
Add the dressing and toss well. Crumble feta and scatter pumpkin seeds on top before serving.

name: Rosti Salad
instructions: Thaw the mini rösti in the fridge. Preheat oven to 220°C.
Crumble the rösti onto a lined baking tray, drizzle with oil, and bake until golden brown per bag directions.
Make the pickled red onion: slice onion into rings, place in a jar with equal parts vinegar and boiling water, 1 tsp salt, and 1 tsp sugar. Let stand 15+ minutes.
Make the yogurt sauce: combine Greek yogurt, dill, mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, chopped gherkins, salt, and pepper.
Make the smashed cucumber: halve the cucumber, scoop out the seeds, smash flat, cut into pieces, and toss with crispy chili oil.
Assemble: layer crispy rösti, yogurt sauce, smashed cucumber, pickled onion, and crumbled feta. Serve immediately.

name: Ultimate Protein Power Bowl
instructions: Cook quinoa according to package directions.
Dice red onion, cucumber, and bell pepper.
Make the chickpea stew: sauté onion and garlic in olive oil. Add cherry tomatoes and smoked paprika. Stir in chickpeas and tomato paste. Simmer 5–7 minutes.

Make the lentil salad: combine drained lentils, diced cucumber, bell pepper, and fresh herbs. Make the balsamic dressing: whisk 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp Dijon mustard, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Toss with lentils.
Make the yogurt-tahini dressing: mix Greek yogurt, tahini, lemon juice, harissa paste, salt, and pepper.
Build the bowls with quinoa, chickpea stew, and lentil salad. Drizzle with yogurt-tahini dressing.

name: Buddha Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Cube sweet potato and eggplant. Place in a baking dish with whole garlic cloves. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, and Chinese five spice. Roast for 20 minutes.
Dice cucumber and apple. Combine with drained lentils and fresh mint in a bowl. Add a squeeze of lime juice.
Cube tofu and pan-fry in a hot skillet with a little oil until golden on all sides.
Make the tahini dressing: combine tahini, roasted garlic cloves (squeezed from skins), ketjap manis, 20 ml water, and lime juice. Stir until smooth.
Build bowls with a base of arugula, topped with the roasted vegetables, lentil-cucumber-apple salad, and crispy tofu. Drizzle with the tahini dressing.

name: Protein-Rich Salad
instructions: Toast flaked almonds in a dry pan until golden. Set aside to cool.
Halve cherry tomatoes, dice avocados, and slice olives and sun-dried tomatoes into pieces.
Drain and rinse lentils. Combine with arugula and all chopped vegetables in a large bowl. Add cottage cheese if using.
Make the dressing: finely dice shallots and mix with red wine vinegar, olive oil, honey, salt, and pepper.
Add the dressing to the salad and toss. Crumble feta on top and scatter toasted almonds. Serve immediately.

name: Roasted Veggies Soup
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Chop sweet potato, bell peppers, and carrots into chunky pieces. Place on a metal baking tray (keep red onion aside).
Drizzle vegetables with olive oil and season generously with salt, pepper, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Roast for 20 minutes, stirring after 10 minutes.
Add sliced red onion to the tray and roast for another 10 minutes.
Transfer all roasted vegetables to a large soup pot. Add vegetable broth and blend until smooth with an immersion blender.
Serve topped with grated Parmesan and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
instructions: Finely dice the onion, carrots, and celery. Heat olive oil in a large pan and sauté the vegetables for 5 minutes.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up as it cooks.
Add the minced garlic, tomato paste, passata, and balsamic vinegar. Stir well.
Season with salt and pepper. Simmer over low heat for 30–45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cook spaghetti in a large pot of salted boiling water until al dente.
Stir in fresh basil and grated Parmesan into the bolognese. Serve over spaghetti.

name: Chicken & Apricot Kofta Bowl
instructions: Mix ground chicken with grated zucchini (squeezed dry), egg, finely chopped dried apricots, ras el hanout, garlic powder, lemon zest, salt, and pepper.
Shape into oval kofta using two spoons. Place on a lined baking tray.
Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until cooked through and golden.
Make the yogurt dressing: combine Greek yogurt, garlic powder, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Make the pickled onion: heat vinegar with 100 ml water and 1 tsp sugar (do not boil). Pour over thinly sliced red onion. Let stand for 10 minutes.
Cook bulgur according to package directions.
Build bowls with bulgur, kofta, pickled onion, halved cherry tomatoes, crumbled feta, and a generous spoon of yogurt dressing.

name: Salmon Bowl with Coconut Rice & Mango Salsa
instructions: Cut salmon into cubes. Rub with olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Roast at 200°C for 9–12 minutes until just cooked through.
Make the mango salsa: dice mango, shred white cabbage, grate carrot, finely dice the red onion. Combine and season with lime juice, honey, salt, and pepper.
Cook the coconut rice: simmer rice with coconut milk, 0.5 cup water, a bay leaf, and a drizzle of olive oil for 15–20 minutes until absorbed.
Make spicy mayo: mix mayonnaise with sriracha and a little honey.
Build bowls with coconut rice, salmon, mango salsa, sliced avocado, and a drizzle of spicy mayo.

name: Korean Style BBQ Beef
instructions: Cut the rib-eye into thin strips.
Blend half the onion (roughly chopped) with garlic, half a pear, spring onions, brown sugar, soy sauce, and sesame oil until smooth.
Pour the marinade over the meat along with the remaining onion (in rings). Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over high heat with olive oil. Cook beef strips in batches for 2–3 minutes until seared and slightly crispy.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and sliced spring onions. Serve over steamed rice.

name: Turkish Pasta
instructions: Cook the farfalle in a large pot of salted boiling water until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Dice the onion. Sauté in olive oil with garlic for 3 minutes.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin, and tomato paste. Season with salt and pepper.
Make garlic yogurt: mix Greek yogurt with 2 minced garlic cloves, salt, and pepper.
Make paprika butter: melt butter in a small pan with smoked paprika until fragrant.
Plate the pasta, top with the beef mixture, a spoonful of garlic yogurt, and a drizzle of paprika butter. Garnish with halved cherry tomatoes, crumbled feta, and fresh parsley.

name: Chicken Meatball Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Cube sweet potato, season with cumin, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Roast for 25–30 minutes.
Toss drained chickpeas with olive oil, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper on a separate tray. Roast alongside the sweet potato.
Make the chicken meatballs: combine ground chicken, egg, breadcrumbs, finely chopped shallot, spring onions, parsley, dill, salt, and pepper. Roll into small balls.
Cook meatballs in a hot oiled skillet until golden and cooked through, about 10–12 minutes.
Make the yogurt-dill dressing: blend Greek yogurt, fresh dill, lime juice, olive oil, maple syrup, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Build bowls with roasted sweet potato, crispy chickpeas, and chicken meatballs. Drizzle generously with dressing.

name: Chili con Carne
instructions: Dice onion, bell pepper, carrot, and celery. Heat olive oil in a large pot and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up as it cooks.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano.
Add tomato sauce, beef broth, salt, and pepper. Simmer over medium heat for 20 minutes, stirring regularly.
Drain and rinse the beans. Add to the chili and cook for another 5 minutes.
Cook rice according to package directions. Serve chili over rice with lime wedges.

name: Tuna Pasta
instructions: Dice the red onion and garlic. Heat olive oil in a large pan and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add sliced red bell pepper, halved cherry tomatoes, capers, and olives. Cook for 5 minutes until tomatoes soften.
Stir in the tomato paste.
Add the passata, drained tuna, and balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 10–15 minutes.
Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente.
Roughly chop feta and basil. Serve tuna sauce over pasta, topped with feta, fresh basil, and a squeeze of lemon.

name: Roasted Veggie & Chicken Mince Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Chop cauliflower and carrots. Season with smoked paprika, garlic powder, turmeric, cinnamon, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Roast for 25–30 minutes.
Cook spelt according to package directions.
Cook ground chicken in a skillet with olive oil, salt, pepper, and a pinch of the same spices until golden and cooked through.
Once spelt is cooked and slightly cooled, drizzle with olive oil and fold in dried cranberries and pomegranate seeds.
Serve the roasted vegetables and seasoned chicken mince over the spelt and pomegranate salad.

name: Cottage Cheese Beef Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Dice sweet potato, season with smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Roast for 20 minutes.
Make the pickled red onion: slice onion thinly and place in a jar with equal parts vinegar and boiling water, 1 tsp salt, and 1 tsp sugar.
Make the cucumber-tomato salad: dice cucumber and halve cherry tomatoes. Toss with a splash of vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Make the cottage cheese-tahini sauce: combine cottage cheese, tahini, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Cook ground beef in a hot skillet with harissa paste, salt, and pepper until browned and slightly crispy.
Build bowls with ground beef, roasted sweet potato, cucumber salad, and cottage cheese-tahini sauce. Top with crumbled feta and pomegranate seeds.

name: Salmon & Spinach Quiche
instructions: Blend the cottage cheese and semi-skimmed milk in a blender until smooth.
Transfer to a bowl and whisk in the eggs until fully combined.
Lightly sauté the spinach in a pan until just wilted, then roughly chop.
Stir in the spinach, finely chopped chives, and sliced smoked salmon. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon zest.
Grease a round baking tin with olive oil. Lay the 2 sheets of puff pastry over the tin to cover the base and sides.
Pour in the filling and crumble feta on top.
Bake at 160°C for 60 minutes until set and lightly golden. Serve with a simple salad.

name: Chicken Mince Bowl with Tzatziki
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C. Slice zucchini and red onion into half moons. Drain and rinse chickpeas.
Toss zucchini, red onion, and chickpeas in a baking dish with olive oil, garlic powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Roast for 20 minutes until golden.

Make the tzatziki: grate cucumber and squeeze out as much water as possible. Finely chop fresh mint and dill. Press the garlic. Combine with Greek yogurt and season with salt, pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Shape ground chicken into meatballs or pan-fry as mince in a hot skillet until golden and cooked through.
Build bowls with roasted vegetables, chicken, and a generous dollop of tzatziki.

name: Honey Mustard Salmon with Rice & Asparagus
instructions: Place salmon fillets in a baking dish. Season with garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Mix Dijon mustard, honey, dill, pressed garlic, and lemon juice. Spread evenly over the salmon. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
Bake at 185°C for 8–9 minutes until the salmon flakes easily.
Trim and halve the asparagus. Toss with olive oil, salt, pepper, grated Parmesan, lemon juice, pressed garlic, and maple syrup.
Roast asparagus at 180°C for 10–12 minutes until tender.
Cook rice according to package directions. Serve salmon and asparagus over rice.

name: Chicken Meatballs Pasta Salad
instructions: Blend zucchini with 3 garlic cloves and lemon zest in a food processor into a fine paste. Squeeze out excess moisture.
Combine the zucchini-garlic paste with ground chicken. Crumble in the feta, season with salt and pepper, and mix well. Form into small meatballs.
Place on a lined baking tray and bake at 190°C for 15–20 minutes until golden and cooked through.

Halve cherry tomatoes. Cook in a pan with 2 pressed garlic cloves for 8–10 minutes until softened. Add tomato paste, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper. Cook 3–5 more minutes, adding a splash of water to loosen into a sauce.
Cook the fusilli until al dente. Drain.
Toss pasta with the tomato sauce and chicken meatballs. Fold in arugula and scatter finely chopped red onion on top.

name: Greek Style Meatball Bowl
instructions: Cut potatoes into wedges. Soak in cold water for a few minutes, then parboil in salted water for 5 minutes. Drain.
Place potatoes in a metal baking dish, drizzle with olive oil, season with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and oregano. Roast at 220°C for 25 minutes, shaking occasionally.
Roughly chop onion and blend with garlic, smoked paprika, oregano, and cumin in a food processor. Combine with ground beef and shape into meatballs. Pan-fry until browned and cooked through.
Make the tzatziki: grate cucumber and squeeze dry. Mix with Greek yogurt, chopped dill, minced garlic, vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Make the Greek salad: chop cucumber, halve cherry tomatoes, slice olives. Dress with olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Serve meatballs with roasted potato wedges, tzatziki, and Greek salad.

name: Viral Dumpling Bake
instructions: Preheat oven to 195°C. Pour coconut milk into a glass baking dish.
Stir in red curry paste, sesame oil, soy sauce, and minced garlic. Mix well.
Add a handful of baby spinach or bok choy.
Arrange frozen dumplings in the dish in a single layer.
Cover tightly with aluminium foil and bake for 20–30 minutes until dumplings are cooked through.
Remove foil, scatter sliced green onions, and drizzle with crispy chili oil.
Serve with steamed rice.

name: Thai Red Curry Chicken with Rice
instructions: Slice red bell pepper into thin strips. Peel garlic and grate ginger.
Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Sear in a hot pan with olive oil until golden on both sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté garlic, ginger, and red pepper for 2 minutes.
Add Thai red curry paste and brown sugar. Cook for 1 minute, then add coconut milk and stir to combine.
Slice the seared chicken and return to the pan. Simmer for 8–10 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in baby spinach and cook until just wilted. Season with lime juice, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Serve over steamed rice.

name: Beef Ricotta Ragout
instructions: Grate the zucchini and squeeze out as much water as possible.
Combine ground beef, grated zucchini, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, ricotta, egg, Parmesan, and breadcrumbs. Mix well and shape into golf-ball sized meatballs.
Bake meatballs at 200°C for 15 minutes.
Roughly chop celery, carrot, and onion and blend in a food processor. Dice red bell pepper.
Sauté the vegetable blend in a wok for 2–3 minutes. Add red bell pepper, garlic, and tomato paste. Cook 2 minutes.
Add passata and balsamic vinegar. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add drained lima beans and simmer for another 10 minutes.
Cook fusilli and serve the ragout sauce and meatballs over the pasta.

name: Korean Beef Bowl
instructions: Make the sauce: combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, gochujang, sesame oil, and cornstarch in a small bowl.
Cook ground beef in a hot skillet until browned and getting slightly crispy. Add the sauce and toss to coat.
Thinly slice cucumber. Make quick pickled onions: cover sliced red onion with apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt and sugar for 10 minutes.
Cook rice according to package directions.
Build bowls with steamed rice, Korean beef, sliced cucumber, and pickled onion. Top with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Raspberry Almond Ricotta Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 170°C. Grease and line a round 23 cm cake tin.
In a large bowl, whisk together ricotta, olive oil, vanilla extract, and sugar until smooth.
Add eggs one at a time, whisking after each addition.
Sift in flour, baking powder, almond flour, lemon zest, and salt. Fold gently until just combined.
Pour batter into the tin. Scatter raspberries evenly on top and sprinkle with sliced almonds.
Bake for 45–50 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes before turning out.

name: Banana Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C. Grease a loaf tin and line with baking paper.
Mash the ripe bananas in a large bowl. Add eggs, coconut flour, vanilla extract, and almond butter. Mix until smooth.
In a separate bowl, combine almond flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
Fold the dry ingredients into the wet until just combined. Stir in walnuts or chocolate chips.
Pour into the prepared loaf tin and bake for 45–50 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool completely before slicing.

name: Matcha Bars
instructions: Add pistachios, cashews, desiccated coconut, oats, matcha powder, pitted dates, almond butter, vanilla, and a pinch of salt to a food processor. Blitz until the mixture clumps together.
Press firmly into a lined baking tin and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in a bain-marie or microwave in 30-second intervals.
Pour melted chocolate over the chilled bars. Sprinkle with extra pistachios, desiccated coconut, and cacao nibs.
Refrigerate until set, then slice into bars. Store in the fridge.

name: Snicker Bar
instructions: Make the base: combine almond flour, oat flour, maple syrup, and coconut oil. Mix until a dough forms. Press into a lined baking tin in an even layer. Freeze for 15 minutes.
Make the date caramel: blend pitted dates, peanut butter, coconut oil, and salt in a food processor until smooth. Spread evenly over the base. Freeze for another 15 minutes.
Melt dark chocolate with 1 tsp coconut oil. Pour over the caramel layer and spread evenly.
Refrigerate until fully set, about 1 hour. Slice into bars and store in the fridge.

name: Date Banana Smoothie
instructions: Pit the dates and peel the banana.
Add all ingredients to a blender along with 300–400 ml of water or milk of choice.
Blend on high for 30–60 seconds until smooth and creamy.
Adjust consistency with more liquid if needed. Serve immediately.

name: Granola
instructions: Preheat oven to 160°C. Mix all dry ingredients — oats, mixed nuts, sunflower seeds, pumpkin seeds, cinnamon, and salt — together in a large bowl.
In a small saucepan, gently warm olive oil, honey, and peanut butter together until combined. Remove from heat.
Let the wet mixture cool slightly, then stir in vanilla extract.
Pour wet ingredients over dry and mix thoroughly until everything is coated.
Spread evenly on a lined baking tray. Bake for 20 minutes, stirring once halfway through.
Let cool completely before storing in an airtight container — it crisps up as it cools.
